summary: postinstall exfiltrates hostname and username to hardcoded bare-IP endpoint
details: package.json declares a postinstall lifecycle script that fires on every `npm install`. The script uses hex-escaped string literals to disguise the names `http`, `os`, `hostname`, and `userInfo`, then issues an HTTP GET to http://208.87.128.25:8888/?h=<hostname>&u=<username>, leaking the installer's host identifiers to a hardcoded bare-IP destination over plaintext HTTP. The package publishes under the @outmarket scope on the public registry and self-describes as a dependency-confusion proof of concept, increasing the risk that internal builds resolving @outmarket/utils will pull this public version instead of an intended private package. Regardless of the 'PoC' framing, the published artifact performs real, unconsented installer-side data exfiltration. The combination of name-squatting against a private scope, hex obfuscation of basic Node built-ins, and a bare-IP HTTP beacon is an unambiguous supply-chain attack fingerprint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
